title: Gorse Bendak
content:

{{Quote|Bendak's the best there ever was, plain and simple. Even now I'd bet that anyone who steps into the ring with him wouldn't make it out alive.|[[Marl]] to [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]|<ref name="kotor" />}}
'''Gorse Bendak''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] former [[Miner/Legends|miner]]-turned [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusader]] who fought in the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Rising to the rank of a [[Field Marshal/Legends|Field Marshal]], Bendak had a great success among the nomadic warrior clans. After the war, he became a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] until a [[Bounty/Legends|fortune posted]] on his head left him unable to work in the trade. Without a job, Bendak turned to his friend and employer [[Ajuur]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who convinced him to wear his [[Neo-Crusader armor|armor]] once more and enter the [[Taris dueling ring|dueling rings]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] under the alias '''Bendak Starkiller'''. Refusing to leave an opponent alive, Bendak retired from the ring after [[Death/Legends|death]] matches were outlawed. During the [[Jedi Civil War]], he remained on Taris, watching matches in the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] and hoping for the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] he could return to the arena for another death match with a worthy opponent.

==Biography==
[[File:BendakStarkiller-KotORCG.png|thumb|left|150px|Bendak Starkiller, Gladiator]]
Bendak was born into a family of miners, a tradition that went back generations that he decided to follow. In the year that the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] invaded the Republic, Bendak was stationed on the mining colony of [[Vanquo]]. Working in the mines on the dayside when the invaders [[Battle of Vanquo|attacked]], he began working on a defense for when they would reach the mines. And sure enough, that defense held the Mandalorians back for two [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]], killing a dozen Mandalorian warriors. Seeing his fighting spirit and abilities, he was offered to join the Mandalorians. Bendak reluctantly agreed, as the only other alternative was [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]].<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

A quick learner in the art of combat and weapon handling, Bendak rose to the rank of Field Marshal in only two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] and received the [[Color/Legends|golden]] armor of the Neo-Crusaders. However, his soldiering career ended when the forces under the command of [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] defeated the Mandalorian forces in the [[Battle of Malachor V|last battle]] of the Mandalorian Wars.

In need of work, Bendak became a skilled bounty hunter, offering his skills to the highest bidder. Bendak found himself with a huge bounty on his head soon after, as he accidentally killed a nobleman's daughter, and was forced to retire the trade and turn to his employer [[Ajuur]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The Hutt encouraged Bendak to become a duelist, using his old armor and adding a stage name to his own: Starkiller. Despite the fact that Mandalorians were hated on the world, Bendak Starkiller became a popular gladiator in the dueling ring—killing many unfortunate opponents. Eventually however, death matches were outlawed and Bendak lost interest, deciding to retire from the rings. He still hoped for a day when a worthy opponent would arrive for one last duel, so he would wander into the cantina to watch the duels. When the duelist [[Ice (duelist)|Ice]], who dreamed of meeting Bendak as a child, finally met him, she considered him to be disgusting.<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide" />

{{GameLS|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
That day eventually came, when the duelist known as the [[Revan/Legends|"Mysterious Stranger"]] became the new champion of Taris. An impressed Bendak came out of his retirement, wanting to duel him in an illegal death match. Revan, however, refused.<ref name="kotorlightside">In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' the death match duel with Bendak is a dark side option</ref>
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Ice dreamed of meeting Bendak when she was a young girl, though found herself disgusted at him when the two finally met.<ref name="kotor" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bendak's [[canon]]ical fate is unknown: assuming that the player picks the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] choice for this scenario, Bendak is not killed by Revan, as dueling with him gives [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] points for breaking the law, despite how vicious a killer Bendak is. If, however, the player picks the dark side choice for the scenario, Revan has the possibility to duel and kill Bendak. In this case, Revan obtains dark side points, the largest purse of credits from the duel, and Bendak's blaster—a modified [[Mandalorian blaster]] pistol that is the strongest standard blaster in the game—is given to Revan by [[Ajuur]] after his death and defeat. Under the argument that Revan risked his life, the player could convince Ajuur to boost the reward from 700 to 900 credits if the Persuade level is high enough. If the persuasion fails, the player could opt to threaten the Hutt for the reward instead, but this will gain more dark side points.

Revan could also get the bounty on Bendak from [[Zax]] in the [[Lower City]].

===Contradictions===
The biographical details given for Bendak Starkiller above, though documented in the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'', contradict information from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', in which Tarisian death matches are said to have been outlawed for "nearly ten years" and Bendak is considered by the other duelists to be an old pro past his prime. As the game is set in [[3956 BBY]], this would have meant that Bendak had been a duelist on Taris in the years leading up to [[3966 BBY/Legends|3966 BBY]], rather than as a miner on Vanquo before it was attacked by Mandalorians two years later.

Zax, the Hutt responsible for running the bounty office at [[Javyar's Cantina]], explains that Bendak acquired the government contract on his head after continuing to kill at illegal underground circuits for some time after the ban on death matches. The campaign guide instead attributes his bounty on having accidentally murdered a nobleman's daughter.

Moreover, though the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] enforced the ban during the game, it was not suggested that it had been they who introduced it, as their conquest and subsequent quarantine of Taris was heavily implied—though not outright stated—to have taken place following the destruction of the ''[[Endar Spire]]''. [[John Jackson Miller]] stated that this tension would be explained in 2009, although that did not happen.<ref name="Faraway-Star Wars Roleplaying Game: Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide">{{Faraway|url=games/swkotorguide.html|text=Star Wars Roleplaying Game: Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide}}</ref>
